actual_output:
## Biography of Glover Teixeira

**Glover Lucas Teixeira** (born October 28, 1979) is a Brazilian-American former professional mixed martial artist who competed in the Light Heavyweight division of the Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC), where he was a former **UFC Light Heavyweight Champion**. He is widely regarded as one of the most accomplished and resilient fighters in UFC history.

### Early Life and Background

Teixeira was born in the rural community of **SobrÃ¡lia, Minas Gerais, Brazil**, and is of Portuguese descent. His hometown was so small that many homes lacked electricity and there was only one gas station. In **1999**, at the age of 19, he immigrated to **Danbury, Connecticut** in the United States to help support his family, where he found work in landscaping.

His interest in combat sports was sparked by watching boxing greats like **Mike Tyson** and early UFC fighters such as **Royce Gracie** and **Chuck Liddell**. After working 10 to 12-hour days in landscaping, he trained in boxing at Hat City Boxing and in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu at **American Top Team Connecticut**. His talent caught the eye of **John Hackleman**, who invited him to train at **The Pit** in California, where Teixeira trained alongside Chuck Liddell.

In **November 2020**, Teixeira became a **naturalized American citizen**. He stated: *"I'm so proud today because I'm officially an American Citizen. Now I'm very proud to represent both Brazil & America."*

### Martial Arts Credentials

Teixeira held several notable ranks:
- **8th degree black belt in Kajukenbo (Hawaiian Kempo)** under John Hackleman
- **2nd degree black belt in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu** under Luigi Mondelli
- **Vale Tudo black belt** under Marco Ruas
- Former member of the **Brazilian National Wrestling Team**
- **ADCC South American Championships**: Gold (2009, -99kg) and Silver (2011, -99kg) in submission wrestling

### Professional MMA Career

Teixeira compiled a professional MMA record of **33 wins and 9 losses** (18 by knockout, 10 by submission, 5 by decision), competing from 2002 to 2023. He fought in organizations including WEC, Impact FC, PFC, and Shooto before joining the UFC.

#### UFC Career Highlights

He debuted in the UFC on **May 26, 2012** at UFC 146, defeating Kyle Kingsbury by first-round submission. He quickly built an impressive winning streak with victories over notable fighters including:

- **Quinton "Rampage" Jackson** (UFC on Fox 6, unanimous decision)
- **James Te Huna** (Submission of the Night)
- **Ryan Bader** (TKO, Knockout of the Night)
- **Ovince Saint Preux** (submission, Fight of the Night)
- **Rashad Evans** (KO, Performance of the Night)
- **Misha Cirkunov** (TKO)
- **Ion CuÈelaba** (submission, Performance of the Night)
- **Anthony Smith** (TKO, Performance of the Night)
- **Thiago Santos** (submission)

#### UFC Light Heavyweight Championship

On **October 30, 2021**, at UFC 267 in Abu Dhabi, Teixeira defeated **Jan BÅachowicz** by rear-naked choke submission in the second round to win the **UFC Light Heavyweight Championship**. At **42 years and 2 days old**, he became:
- The **oldest first-time champion in UFC history**
- The **oldest fighter to win the UFC Light Heavyweight Championship**
- The **second oldest UFC champion** in history (behind Randy Couture)

He lost the title to **JiÅÃ­ ProchÃ¡zka** at UFC 275 on June 11, 2022, via fifth-round rear-naked choke submission in what was widely considered the **2022 Fight of the Year**.

His final fight came on **January 21, 2023**, at UFC 283, where he lost a unanimous decision to **Jamahal Hill** for the vacant UFC Light Heavyweight Championship. He announced his **retirement** during the post-fight interview.

### UFC Records and Accomplishments

Teixeira set numerous UFC Light Heavyweight division records:
- **Most post-fight bonuses** in LHW division history (10)
- **Most finishes** in LHW division history (13)
- **Most submissions** in LHW division history (7)
- **Most submission attempts** in LHW division history (16)
- **Second most wins** in LHW division history (16)
- Four-time **Performance of the Night** winner
- Four-time **Fight of the Night** winner
- One-time **Knockout of the Night** winner
- One-time **Submission of the Night** winner

He received numerous media accolades, including **2020 Comeback Fighter of the Year** from multiple outlets and the universally recognized **2022 Fight of the Year** (vs. ProchÃ¡zka) from ESPN, Sherdog, Combat Press, World MMA Awards, and others.

### Activism and Philanthropy

Beyond fighting, Teixeira was active in his community and beyond:
- **2013**: Testified before the Connecticut General Assembly to advocate for the legalization and regulation of MMA in Connecticut
- **2013**: Participated in UFC: Fight for the Troops 3, supporting the Intrepid Fallen Heroes Fund
- **2014**: Met with Senators John McCain and Harry Reid at the U.S. Capitol to support brain health research for fighters at the Cleveland Clinic's Lou R
